=== results.tex lines 4-60 (budget prose) ===
\subsection{The per-sightline dispersion and scattering budget}
\label{sec:results-budget}

Applying the decomposition of Section~\ref{sec:dm} (Eq.~\ref{eq:dmbudget}) to the
twelve sightlines yields the budget of Table~\ref{tab:budget} and
Figure~\ref{fig:budget}. The Galactic term is modest and similar across the
sample ($\mathrm{DM_{MW}}=78$--$171\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$), while the
cosmological mean grows with redshift to dominate the higher-$z$ sightlines. An
intervening circumgalactic column is significant only where a sightline passes
through a foreground galaxy's interior: three sightlines (FRB 20220310F, FRB 20230307A,
FRB 20230814B) fall in this galaxy-interior regime, where $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$ is
capped at $b=0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$ (the uncapped core-extrapolated columns reach
$364$--$712\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$), while most sightlines do not intersect a
confirmed foreground system and carry no intervening term. The host contribution
is the residual after the modeled terms are removed; for four sightlines (FRB 20220207C,
FRB 20220310F, FRB 20221203A, FRB 20230307A) it is formally negative---the modeled terms
over-predict $\mathrm{DM_{obs}}$. This is concentrated in the galaxy-interior
sightlines, where the capped circumgalactic column is most uncertain, and a
prior-predictive sensitivity analysis quantifies the probability of a negative
host term per sightline. Three sightlines (FRB 20230325A, FRB 20240122A, FRB 20230814B) lack a
spectroscopic host redshift and are carried only through the
redshift-independent Galactic term.

The predicted intervening scattering spans
$\tau_{\mathrm{int}}\sim10^{-5}$--$1.4\,\mathrm{ms}$ at $1\,\mathrm{GHz}$, largest
for the galaxy-interior sightlines (Figure~\ref{fig:budget}, right). A direct
measured-versus-predicted comparison draws on the uniform multi-component
re-fit of Section~\ref{sec:results-alpha}: its eight well-constrained sightlines
now yield a measured $(\tau_{1\,\mathrm{GHz}},\alpha)$ (Table~\ref{tab:alpha}),
while the marginal sightlines are withheld as unconstrained. We show the
predicted intervening contribution here (Figure~\ref{fig:budget}); overlaying the
measured $\tau_{1\,\mathrm{GHz}}$ on it is the principal remaining visualization
step for the sample.

\input{budget_table.tex}

\begin{figure*}
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=0.95\textwidth]{sightline_dm_scattering_budget}
  \caption{Per-sightline dispersion and scattering budget for the twelve
  co-detections. \emph{Left:} the observed dispersion measure (black bar) of each
  burst decomposed into the Milky-Way ISM (NE2001) and halo, the cosmological mean
  (Macquart relation), the capped intervening circumgalactic column
  ($b<0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$), and the host residual; the placeholder-redshift
  sightlines (FRB 20230325A, FRB 20240122A, FRB 20230814B) show only the redshift-independent Galactic
  term. \emph{Right:} the predicted intervening pulse-broadening at $1\,\mathrm{GHz}$
  per sightline; the per-sightline measured $\tau$ from the uniform re-fit
  (Table~\ref{tab:alpha}, Section~\ref{sec:results-alpha}) are not yet overlaid on
  this figure, so only the predicted contribution is shown here.
  Generated by the \texttt{dsa110-FLITS} pipeline
  (\texttt{galaxies/v2\_0/sightline\_budget.py}).}
  \label{fig:budget}
\end{figure*}

\subsection{Scattering index and burst multiplicity}
\label{sec:results-alpha}

content.text:
Now I see the true blast radius: the committed manuscript narrates the **old** budget state throughout — abstract (main.tex:33), results.tex:13-14,18, discussion.tex:16-17, conclusions.tex:14-15 all name the interior sightlines as Whitney/Phineas/**Mahi**. Adopting photoz-fix swaps Mahi→**Casey** and changes the negative-DM_host list. This is a manuscript-wide factual reconciliation, not a local insert.

Before cascading any prose, I must confirm the photoz-fix budget regenerates deterministically. Let me verify reproduction.

REPO="/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS"
sys.path.insert(0, REPO)

df["name"]=[load_tns_name(n) for n in df["name"]]

\begin{deluxetable*}{lrrrrrllrr}
\tabletypesize{\scriptsize}
\tablecaption{Per-sightline dispersion and scattering budget for the twelve
co-detections, from the decomposition of Eq.~\ref{eq:dmbudget}. $\mathrm{DM_{MW}}$
is the NE2001 disk plus a $40\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ halo; $\langle\mathrm{DM_{cos}}\rangle$
is the Macquart mean at the host redshift; $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$ is the intervening
circumgalactic column (capped at $b=0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$); $\mathrm{DM_{host}}$ is
the residual; $\tau_{\mathrm{int}}$ is the predicted intervening pulse-broadening
at $1\,\mathrm{GHz}$. ``regime'' and ``mass'' give the intervening column's
geometry (CGM, galaxy-interior, or none) and whether its halo mass is measured or
assumed. No PASS-grade measured $\tau$ is available, so the scattering comparison
is predicted-only (Figure~\ref{fig:budget}). DM in $\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$.

file.content:
for ax, (name, tns, ra, dec, z_frb) in zip(axes.ravel(), GAL_TARGETS):
        ax.set_facecolor(BG_LIGHT)
        d = dominant_foreground_halo(name, ra, dec, z_frb, gal_dir)
        rvir, b, mh, zg = d["r_vir"], d["impact_kpc"], d["m_halo"], d["z_gal"]
        b_cap = INTERIOR_B_OVER_RVIR * rvir
        interior = d["b_over_rvir"] < INTERIOR_B_OVER_RVIR

        # mNFW projected hot-halo DM as a function of impact parameter.
        bb = np.linspace(0.5, rvir, 240)
        dm_b = np.array([scat.dm_halo_mnfw(mh, zg, float(x)) or 0.0 for x in bb])
        ax.plot(bb, dm_b, color=HALO_COLOR, lw=2.2, zorder=4, label="hot mNFW column")

        # Interior region (b < 0.1 R_vir): the smooth column is extrapolated and
        # capped at the 0.1 R_vir floor.
        ax.axvspan(0, b_cap, color=HOST_COLOR, alpha=0.07, zorder=0)
        ax.axvline(
            b_cap, color=HOST_COLOR, ls=":", lw=1.4, zorder=3, label="$0.1\\,R_{\\rm vir}$ cap"
        )
        ax.axvline(rvir, color=TEXT_DARK, ls="--", lw=1.0, alpha=0.6, zorder=3)
        ax.text(
            rvir,
            ax.get_ylim()[1] * 0.02,
            "$R_{\\rm vir}$",
            fontsize=7,
            color=TEXT_DARK,
            ha="right",
            va="bottom",
            rotation=90,
        )

        # The sightline's actual impact and the DM it samples.
        dm_at_b = scat.dm_halo_mnfw(mh, zg, b) or 0.0
        ax.axvline(b, color=INTERV_COLOR, lw=2.0, zorder=5)
        ax.scatter([b], [dm_at_b], color=INTERV_COLOR, s=42, zorder=6, edgecolor="white")
        ax.annotate(
            f"$b={b:.1f}$ kpc",
            xy=(b, dm_at_b),
            xytext=(8, 6),
            textcoords="offset points",
            fontsize=8,

            f"DM$={dm_at_b:.0f}$",
            xy=(b, dm_at_b),
            xytext=(8, 6),
            textcoords="offset points",
            fontsize=8,
            color=INTERV_COLOR,
            fontweight="bold",
        )

        regime = "interior" if interior else "CGM grazer"
        info = (
            f"$z={zg:.3f}$,  $\\log M_{{\\rm halo}}={d['logM_halo']:.1f}$\n"
            f"$b={b:.1f}$ kpc,  $b/R_{{\\rm vir}}={d['b_over_rvir']:.3f}$ ({regime})\n"
            f"halo DM$={d['dm_halo']:.0f}\\!\\to\\!{dm_cap:.0f}$ (capped)\n"
            f"$\\tau_{{1\\rm GHz}}={d['tau']:.2g}$ ms,  {_mass_label(d['mass_source'])}"
        )

\input{foreground_table.tex}

\subsection{The dominant intervening systems}
\label{sec:grand-systems}

Of the confirmed foreground census, only a handful of sightlines thread a
foreground halo closely enough to source a significant intervening column. Three
sightlines pass through a foreground galaxy's interior ($b<0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$),
and a fourth grazes the outer circumgalactic medium. Figure~\ref{fig:grand_galaxies}
characterizes these four dominant intervening galaxies: for each, the projected hot
modified-NFW dispersion column \citep{ProchaskaZheng2019} is shown as a function of
impact parameter, with the sightline's actual impact, the $R_{\mathrm{vir}}$
truncation, and the $b=0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$ floor at which the smooth column is
capped before it is extrapolated through the galaxy interior. The deepest crossing
is FRB~20230307A, whose sightline passes $b\approx7.5$\,kpc
($b/R_{\mathrm{vir}}\approx0.04$) from a spectroscopically-confirmed
$\log(M_{\mathrm{halo}}/M_\odot)\approx12.1$ halo at $z\approx0.23$; its capped
intervening column $\mathrm{DM_{int}}=471\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ (raw
$733\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ uncapped) and predicted $\tau_{1\,\mathrm{GHz}}\approx1.9$\,ms
are the largest in the sample. FRB~20220310F ($b\approx5.4$\,kpc, capped
$200\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$, $\tau\approx0.54$\,ms) and FRB~20240229A ($b\approx12$\,kpc,
capped $207\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$, $\tau\approx0.47$\,ms) are the other two interior
crossings, while FRB~20221113A grazes its foreground halo at
$b/R_{\mathrm{vir}}\approx0.14$ (just outside the interior cap) for a smaller
$\mathrm{DM_{int}}\approx112\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$. Each panel plots the dominant
halo; the per-sightline $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$ of Table~\ref{tab:budget} additionally
sums the lesser foreground halos along the same sightline.

\begin{figure*}
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=0.98\textwidth]{grand_galaxies_cgm}
  \caption{The four dominant intervening galaxies. For each sightline the hot
  modified-NFW circumgalactic dispersion column \citep{ProchaskaZheng2019} of the
  dominant foreground halo is plotted against projected impact parameter $b$ (blue);
  the orange line marks the sightline's actual impact and the dispersion the halo
  contributes there, the dotted red line the $b=0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$ floor below
  which the smooth column is capped (shaded interior region), and the dashed line
  $R_{\mathrm{vir}}$. Annotations give the halo redshift, mass, scaled impact and
  regime, the raw$\to$capped hot column, the predicted $1\,\mathrm{GHz}$
  pulse-broadening, and the stellar-mass provenance. Three sightlines
  (FRB~20230307A, FRB~20220310F, FRB~20240229A) pierce the galaxy interior; FRB~20221113A
  grazes the outer CGM. Generated by the \texttt{dsa110-FLITS} pipeline
  (\texttt{galaxies/v2\_0/grand\_systems\_figures.py}).}
  \label{fig:grand_galaxies}
\end{figure*}

The same FRB~20230307A field that hosts the deepest galaxy crossing is also the
only one whose sightline threads a foreground cluster environment: it carries eight
spectroscopically-confirmed foreground clusters (Table~\ref{tab:foreground}), of
which Figure~\ref{fig:grand_clusters} characterizes the four innermost by an
isothermal $\beta$-model intracluster-medium column normalized to the cluster
gas mass within $R_{500}$. Only the nearest, J115120.4+714435
($z=0.200$, $\log(M_{500}/M_\odot)\approx14.1$), lies inside its own $R_{500}$
($b/R_{500}=0.83$), where the $\beta$-model gives an intracluster dispersion ceiling
of $\lesssim180\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$; the next, J115128.2+713637, passes just
outside at $1.25\,R_{500}$ ($\lesssim80\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$), and the remaining two
cross at $3$--$3.3\,R_{500}$, beyond the model truncation, contributing negligibly.
The isothermal $\beta$-model holds its density slope to large radius and so
over-predicts the projected column by a factor of a few relative to the resolved
cluster profiles; the values are therefore conservative upper bounds, not point
estimates. This potential intracluster column is not folded into the galaxy
circumgalactic $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$ of Table~\ref{tab:budget}; were the innermost
cluster's contribution real it would only deepen the already-negative host residual
on the FRB~20230307A sightline, which---taken together with the core-extrapolated
galaxy interior---bounds rather than sources the observed dispersion there.

\begin{figure*}
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=0.98\textwidth]{grand_clusters_icm}
  \caption{The four innermost foreground clusters of the FRB~20230307A field. For
  each cluster the isothermal $\beta$-model intracluster-medium dispersion column,
  normalized to the hot-gas mass within $R_{500}$, is plotted against impact
  parameter $b$ (blue); $R_{500}$ is the dashed line (shaded interior) and the
  solid line the sightline's impact, red where it falls inside $R_{500}$ and orange
  where outside. Only J115120.4+714435 (top left) lies inside $R_{500}$
  ($b/R_{500}=0.83$); the others pass at $1.25$--$3.3\,R_{500}$. $R_{500}$ and
  $M_{500}$ are recovered from the tabulated impact and $b/R_{500}$ at the cluster
  redshift. The $\beta$-model column is a conservative upper bound (it over-predicts
  the outskirts). Generated by the \texttt{dsa110-FLITS} pipeline
  (\texttt{galaxies/v2\_0/grand\_systems\_figures.py}).}
  \label{fig:grand_clusters}
\end{figure*}

\subsection{Scattering Attribution}

+  normalized to the hot-gas mass within $R_{500}$, is plotted against impact
+  parameter $b$ (blue); $R_{500}$ is the dashed line (shaded interior) and the
+  solid line the sightline's impact, red where it falls inside $R_{500}$ and orange
+  where outside. Only J115120.4+714435 (top left) lies inside $R_{500}$

+  redshift. The $\beta$-model column is a conservative upper bound (it over-predicts

=== results.tex 10-31 ===
sample ($\mathrm{DM_{MW}}=78$--$171\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$), while the
cosmological mean grows with redshift to dominate the higher-$z$ sightlines. An
intervening circumgalactic column is significant only where a sightline passes
through a foreground galaxy's interior: three sightlines (FRB 20220310F, FRB 20230307A,
FRB 20230814B) fall in this galaxy-interior regime, where $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$ is
capped at $b=0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$ (the uncapped core-extrapolated columns reach
$364$--$712\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$), while most sightlines do not intersect a
confirmed foreground system and carry no intervening term. The host contribution
is the residual after the modeled terms are removed; for four sightlines (FRB 20220207C,
FRB 20220310F, FRB 20221203A, FRB 20230307A) it is formally negative---the modeled terms
over-predict $\mathrm{DM_{obs}}$. This is concentrated in the galaxy-interior
sightlines, where the capped circumgalactic column is most uncertain, and a
prior-predictive sensitivity analysis quantifies the probability of a negative
host term per sightline. Three sightlines (FRB 20230325A, FRB 20240122A, FRB 20230814B) lack a
spectroscopic host redshift and are carried only through the
redshift-independent Galactic term.

The predicted intervening scattering spans
$\tau_{\mathrm{int}}\sim10^{-5}$--$1.4\,\mathrm{ms}$ at $1\,\mathrm{GHz}$, largest
for the galaxy-interior sightlines (Figure~\ref{fig:budget}, right). A direct
measured-versus-predicted comparison draws on the uniform multi-component
re-fit of Section~\ref{sec:results-alpha}: its eight well-constrained sightlines
=== discussion.tex 8-25 ===
diffractive bandwidth lies $\sim$7--11$\times$ below the NE2025 prediction, and
never above it, so the dominant scattering screen is not the Milky Way. For the
prototype FRB~20221203A the three separated scintillation scales
(Figure~\ref{fig:scint_screens})---a Galactic floor far above and a
pulse-broadening screen far below the resolved diffractive scale---localize that
screen to the host or an intervening medium rather than the Galaxy.

Whether that screen is intervening or intrinsic to the host is, in principle,
decided by the foreground census. The three sightlines that pierce a foreground
galaxy's interior (FRB~20220310F, FRB~20230307A, FRB~20230814B) carry both the
largest predicted intervening dispersion and the largest predicted intervening
pulse-broadening (Figure~\ref{fig:budget}, right), and are the natural candidates
for an intervening scattering screen. The high-amplitude scintillation-excess
sightlines, however, are not in general the galaxy-interior ones, so the present
evidence favors a host-dominated screen for the strongly scattered bursts, with
an intervening screen remaining viable only where a confirmed foreground galaxy
lies at small impact parameter. Discriminating the two requires the
measured-versus-predicted $\tau$ comparison that Figure~\ref{fig:budget} sets up
=== conclusions.tex 10-20 ===
($\mathrm{DM_{MW}}=78$--$171\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$) and a cosmological mean that
grows with redshift to dominate the higher-$z$ bursts, with no intervening
contribution required (Section~\ref{sec:results-budget}).
\item An intervening circumgalactic column is significant only for the three
sightlines that pass through a foreground galaxy's interior
($b<0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$); on four galaxy-interior sightlines the host residual
is formally negative, which bounds the most uncertain (capped) circumgalactic
model rather than the host (Section~\ref{sec:foreground}).
\item A foreground search of the twelve fields returns 49 candidate systems
(29 confirmed foreground), establishing which sightlines can carry an intervening
dispersion or scattering term (Table~\ref{tab:foreground}).

sample ($\mathrm{DM_{MW}}=78$--$171\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$), while the
cosmological mean grows with redshift to dominate the higher-$z$ sightlines. An
intervening circumgalactic column is significant only where a sightline passes
through a foreground galaxy's interior: three sightlines (FRB 20220310F, FRB 20230307A,
FRB 20230814B) fall in this galaxy-interior regime, where $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$ is
capped at $b=0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$ (the uncapped core-extrapolated columns reach
$364$--$712\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$), while most sightlines do not intersect a
confirmed foreground system and carry no intervening term. The host contribution
is the residual after the modeled terms are removed; for four sightlines (FRB 20220207C,
FRB 20220310F, FRB 20221203A, FRB 20230307A) it is formally negative---the modeled terms
over-predict $\mathrm{DM_{obs}}$. This is concentrated in the galaxy-interior
sightlines, where the capped circumgalactic column is most uncertain, and a
prior-predictive sensitivity analysis quantifies the probability of a negative
host term per sightline. Three sightlines (FRB 20230325A, FRB 20240122A, FRB 20230814B) lack a
spectroscopic host redshift and are carried only through the
redshift-independent Galactic term.

The predicted intervening scattering spans
$\tau_{\mathrm{int}}\sim10^{-5}$--$1.4\,\mathrm{ms}$ at $1\,\mathrm{GHz}$, largest
for the galaxy-interior sightlines (Figure~\ref{fig:budget}, right). A direct
measured-versus-predicted comparison draws on the uniform multi-component
re-fit of Section~\ref{sec:results-alpha}: its eight well-constrained sightlines
now yield a measured $(\tau_{1\,\mathrm{GHz}},\alpha)$ (Table~\ref{tab:alpha}),
while the marginal sightlines are withheld as unconstrained. We show the
predicted intervening contribution here (Figure~\ref{fig:budget}); overlaying the
measured $\tau_{1\,\mathrm{GHz}}$ on it is the principal remaining visualization
step for the sample.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
\section{Discussion}
\label{sec:discussion}

The per-sightline budget lets us ask, for each burst, whether its scattering can
be assigned to a specific screen. The clearest evidence for an extragalactic
screen is the diffractive-scintillation excess of Section~\ref{sec:scattering}:
on the mid-latitude sightlines where the Galactic floor is reliable, the measured
diffractive bandwidth lies $\sim$7--11$\times$ below the NE2025 prediction, and
never above it, so the dominant scattering screen is not the Milky Way. For the
prototype FRB~20221203A the three separated scintillation scales
(Figure~\ref{fig:scint_screens})---a Galactic floor far above and a
pulse-broadening screen far below the resolved diffractive scale---localize that
screen to the host or an intervening medium rather than the Galaxy.

Whether that screen is intervening or intrinsic to the host is, in principle,
decided by the foreground census. The three sightlines that pierce a foreground
galaxy's interior (FRB~20220310F, FRB~20230307A, FRB~20230814B) carry both the
largest predicted intervening dispersion and the largest predicted intervening
pulse-broadening (Figure~\ref{fig:budget}, right), and are the natural candidates
for an intervening scattering screen. The high-amplitude scintillation-excess
sightlines, however, are not in general the galaxy-interior ones, so the present
evidence favors a host-dominated screen for the strongly scattered bursts, with
an intervening screen remaining viable only where a confirmed foreground galaxy
lies at small impact parameter. Discriminating the two requires the
measured-versus-predicted $\tau$ comparison that Figure~\ref{fig:budget} sets up
but cannot yet close until the per-sightline $\tau$ set is finalized into the
overlay; this is the test we identify as the principal remaining measurement for
the sample.

\subsection{Burst multiplicity as a systematic on the scattering index}
\label{sec:disc-alpha}

The result of Section~\ref{sec:results-alpha} is a cautionary systematic for any
single-component scattering index. A temporal sub-component omitted from the model
is partially absorbed into the scattering tail and biases $\alpha$---classically
high, as a steeper slope and a larger scattering time mimic the unmodeled flux,
but the bias can instead rail the fit against a prior bound when the multiplicity
is split across the two bands, as in FRB~20220310F where an unmodeled DSA-110
sub-pulse drives $\alpha$ to its \emph{lower} bound. Because $\alpha$ distinguishes
a Kolmogorov turbulent screen ($\alpha\simeq4.4$; \citealp{Rickett1990}) from a
shallower spectrum, an unrecognized companion can masquerade as anomalous
scattering---a regime in which measured pulse-broadening indices already scatter
well below the Kolmogorov value \citep{Bhat2004}. We verified that a favored
second component is not an artifact of the likelihood: its evidence gain is flat
across the gain-prior scale (so it is not prior-driven), reconstructing the
single-component residual reproduces the whiteness statistic that flagged the
burst, and the companion sits well inside the resolvable separation.

A second systematic is the assumed PBF. A band-dependent PBF
shape---formally favored for some bursts but unphysical for a single
sightline---can shift a marginal, poorly-constrained burst's $\alpha$ by several
tenths, while leaving the well-constrained sightlines unchanged at the $\le0.1$
level (Section~\ref{sec:jointfit}). Multiplicity and PBF must therefore both be
controlled before a per-sightline $\alpha$ is interpreted; the indices reported
here come from a uniform re-fit of the sample under a single physical (exponential)
PBF, modeling temporal multiplicity per band. For the marginal sightlines---those
whose posterior rails or reaches a prior bound under either choice---we quote no
index, treating them as unconstrained rather than anomalous.

\section{Conclusions}
\label{sec:conclusions}

We have assembled the twelve CHIME/FRB--DSA-110 co-detected FRBs and used their
$\sim$1\,GHz detection baseline to separate, sightline by sightline, the media
that contribute to the observed dispersion and scattering.

\begin{enumerate}
\item The observed DM decomposes for most sightlines into a modest Milky-Way term
($\mathrm{DM_{MW}}=78$--$171\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$) and a cosmological mean that
grows with redshift to dominate the higher-$z$ bursts, with no intervening
contribution required (Section~\ref{sec:results-budget}).
\item An intervening circumgalactic column is significant only for the three
sightlines that pass through a foreground galaxy's interior
($b<0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$); on four galaxy-interior sightlines the host residual
is formally negative, which bounds the most uncertain (capped) circumgalactic
model rather than the host (Section~\ref{sec:foreground}).
\item A foreground search of the twelve fields returns 49 candidate systems
(29 confirmed foreground), establishing which sightlines can carry an intervening
dispersion or scattering term (Table~\ref{tab:foreground}).
\item A diffractive-scintillation excess of $\sim$7--11$\times$ the NE2025
Galactic floor on the mid-latitude sightlines, robust across
scattering-calibrated electron-density models, points to an extragalactic (host
or intervening) scattering screen rather than a Milky-Way origin
(Section~\ref{sec:scattering}).
\item Modeling hidden temporal sub-components is essential to an unbiased
scattering index: an unmodeled companion is absorbed into the scattering tail and
biases $\alpha$ away from its true value (Section~\ref{sec:disc-alpha}).
\item For the eight sightlines with a spectroscopic host redshift we measure
band-restricted isotropic-equivalent energies of
$5\times10^{38}$--$1\times10^{41}\,\mathrm{erg}$ (Table~\ref{tab:burst-energies}).
\end{enumerate}

The principal step still outstanding is to finalize the per-sightline scattering
time and complete the measured-versus-predicted overlay of
Figure~\ref{fig:budget}: the uniform multi-component re-fit already constrains the
clean single-screen sightlines, and locking the marginal ones will turn the
predicted intervening pulse-broadening into a direct, per-sightline test of where
each burst is scattered.

1	# Deferred-task ledger
2

5	item tagged `@agent` remains — work the agent can do itself. Policy:
6	[CLAUDE.md → "Deferred tasks gate completion"](../CLAUDE.md).
7	
8	Tags (exactly one per item):
9	- `@agent` — the agent can execute/implement it now → **blocks** completion until done.
10	- `@human` — needs a person or a one-way door (push/publish/PR) → does not block.
11	- `@decision` — a product/science choice is pending → does not block.
12	- `@separate-lane` — belongs to another task's git lane → does not block.
13	
14	To clear an `@agent` item: finish it and change `- [ ]` to `- [x]`. Only retag to a
15	non-blocking tag if it genuinely cannot be done by the agent now.
16	
17	## Open
18	
19	These are the residual *execution* items behind the decision-map answers
20	([`docs/rse/specs/decision-map-manuscript-completion.md`](../docs/rse/specs/decision-map-manuscript-completion.md),
21	all 7 tickets resolved 2026-06-24). The science decisions are recorded in
22	ADR-0002/0003/0004; what remains is data-dependent regeneration, the active
23	scattering lane, and the separate Faber2026 repo — none agent-completable in a
24	docs close-out, so none are `@agent`.

The verdicts were **deliberately NOT regenerated** — per Codex/astro adjudication, regen must wait for the canonical all-exp fit set, not run on mixed data. **Remaining @decision:** the actual verdict regeneration is blocked on the canonical-fit-set choice (#4 citable-α lock); only johndoeII's FAIL→MARGINAL flip is scientifically sound, oran (true all-exp α≈2.69) and whitney (true ≈5.12) must NOT be relabeled sub-Kolmogorov on their mixed 1.44/1.46.

Also fixed the `_s2-<n>_pbf-…` tag-parse crash and added a PBF column to `_ladder.py`; `test_s2verdict.py` self-check passes and encodes the zach mixed-vs-all-exp verdict flip. **Remaining @human:** pull the canonical all-exp fixed-s² grid from HPCC (`/central/scratch/jfaber/flits-runs/.../*_s2-*_pbf-exp-exp.json`) so the default path can actually adjudicate zach C2D3 (today it correctly refuses — the grid is not local), and optionally delete the 48 stale mixed-PBF `*_s2-*.json` under the deletion-safety gate. **Guard landed via PR #57** (`fix/s2-pbf-guard`, cherry-picked `1c87ef5` off `origin/main` + a portable-test fix) — only the HPCC grid pull above remains before the default path can adjudicate zach C2D3.

# Deferred-task ledger

Open follow-ups carried by a session. The deferred-task Stop gate
(`.claude/hooks/deferred-task-gate.sh`) blocks end-of-turn while any **unchecked**
item tagged `@agent` remains — work the agent can do itself. Policy:
[CLAUDE.md → "Deferred tasks gate completion"](../CLAUDE.md).

Tags (exactly one per item):
- `@agent` — the agent can execute/implement it now → **blocks** completion until done.
- `@human` — needs a person or a one-way door (push/publish/PR) → does not block.
- `@decision` — a product/science choice is pending → does not block.
- `@separate-lane` — belongs to another task's git lane → does not block.

To clear an `@agent` item: finish it and change `- [ ]` to `- [x]`. Only retag to a
non-blocking tag if it genuinely cannot be done by the agent now.

## Open

These are the residual *execution* items behind the decision-map answers
([`docs/rse/specs/decision-map-manuscript-completion.md`](../docs/rse/specs/decision-map-manuscript-completion.md),
all 7 tickets resolved 2026-06-24). The science decisions are recorded in
ADR-0002/0003/0004; what remains is data-dependent regeneration, the active
scattering lane, and the separate Faber2026 repo — none agent-completable in a
docs close-out, so none are `@agent`.

- [ ] Implement the **L1 sub-Kolmogorov floor** (ADR-0004). **Policy + guard landed 2026-06-24** (Codex-adjudicated option 2 — the data-independent half ships, the regeneration is held): `gate_joint_committed.py` floor `ALPHA_MIN` 1.5 → 1.0, 1.0 ≤ α < 2.0 → L3 "sub-Kolmogorov (inspect)" MARGINAL, plus the 3σ-of-prior-bound rail rule and a **fail-closed PBF guard** in `main()` that *refuses* to regenerate verdicts from the 11/11 mixed-PBF `joint_json/` set (their α are superseded by ADR-0003; regrading them would leak a known-wrong sub-K label for oran/whitney into the energy/citable path) unless `FLITS_GATE_ALLOW_MIXED=1` stamps them NON-CITABLE; reconciled the dead `ALPHA_MARGINAL_MIN` 2.0 → 1.0 and the `AGENT_CONFIGURATION_FLITS.md` prose; `test_gate_joint_committed.py` updated (13 passing). The verdicts were **deliberately NOT regenerated** — per Codex/astro adjudication, regen must wait for the canonical all-exp fit set, not run on mixed data. **Remaining @decision:** the actual verdict regeneration is blocked on the canonical-fit-set choice (#4 citable-α lock); only johndoeII's FAIL→MARGINAL flip is scientifically sound, oran (true all-exp α≈2.69) and whitney (true ≈5.12) must NOT be relabeled sub-Kolmogorov on their mixed 1.44/1.46.
- [ ] Fix the **s²-grid reproducibility hazard** (ADR-0003). **Guard landed 2026-06-24** — `_s2verdict.py` is now fail-closed: it adjudicates only the all-exp PBF family by default, excludes the legacy mixed-PBF JSONs (which omit `pbf_C/pbf_D`), and *refuses* to render a verdict when no all-exp fixed-s² grid is present rather than silently using the mixed grid (set `FLITS_S2_PBF=mixed` to inspect the non-canonical legacy grid explicitly). Also fixed the `_s2-<n>_pbf-…` tag-parse crash and added a PBF column to `_ladder.py`; `test_s2verdict.py` self-check passes and encodes the zach mixed-vs-all-exp verdict flip. **Remaining @human:** pull the canonical all-exp fixed-s² grid from HPCC (`/central/scratch/jfaber/flits-runs/.../*_s2-*_pbf-exp-exp.json`) so the default path can actually adjudicate zach C2D3 (today it correctly refuses — the grid is not local), and optionally delete the 48 stale mixed-PBF `*_s2-*.json` under the deletion-safety gate. **Guard landed via PR #57** (`fix/s2-pbf-guard`, cherry-picked `1c87ef5` off `origin/main` + a portable-test fix) — only the HPCC grid pull above remains before the default path can adjudicate zach C2D3.
- [ ] **Faber2026 Fork-B consistency pass** on the separate repo (`~/Developer/overleaf/Faber2026`): apply the figure↔text decisions (#7) — quote all-exp freya α=4.356, recolor/withdraw zach from "well-constrained", labels from `burst_metadata` — and keep the citable-α roster count-free until #4 locks. **@human** — separate repo + PR (one-way door); gated on the citable-α set (#4) finalizing.
- [ ] Reconcile the **manuscript energies table** (`~/Developer/overleaf/Faber2026` `sections/results.tex` `tab:burst-energies`, abstract "eight … energies") to the canonical energies sample once #4 locks, and add the per-section exclusion caption (no spectroscopic z / FAIL-gated joint fit / no joint c0,γ fit). **@decision** — the selection *rule* is decided (decision-map #6: spec-z + quality-passing DSA fluence + energy-calc data, per-section sample rule), but the exact row set (6-row #42 by nickname vs 8-row manuscript by TNS; zach suspect per #5) is the open science call, gated on the citable-α set (#4). Surfaced in Faber2026 PR #9.
- [ ] Reconcile the **ADR-contradicting campaign counts** in `docs/codetection-science-plan.md` (line ~37 "11/12 joint scattering fits" vs casey-landed 12/12; line ~79 "Profile-bias α case study: zach 3.32→2.41") and `docs/rse/specs/plan-manuscript-completion.md` ("adversarially trusted α 3/12") against the now-committed ADRs: ADR-0003 supersedes all mixed-PBF α and moves the profile-bias demonstrator off **zach** onto **whitney**; ADR-0004 reclassifies 1.0 ≤ α < 2.0 as MARGINAL. The pure stub-framing references were corrected in-place 2026-06-24; these count/roster lines were deliberately left because their *corrected* values depend on the HPCC zach all-exp grid pull and the citable-α lock (#4). **@decision** — do not invent the corrected counts; reconcile once the citable-α set finalizes.

- [ ] **Place the all-exp joint figures into the manuscript** (`dsa_figs/` → Faber2026). **Generation DONE 2026-06-24** (user-authorized "full unblock"): the canonical all-exp `tau_nu_ladder_allexp` + `joint_ppc_montage_allexp` were rendered for the **seven publishable bursts** (ALLEXP_PBF_RUN.md verdict: freya, casey, chromatica, wilhelm, oran, phineas, whitney; johndoeII/hamilton/isha/mahi/zach excluded — railed/PBF-unstable). New generators in the refit lane (untracked): `joint_ladder/_tau_ladder_allexp.py` (npz-only, ladder needs only the shared `tau_1ghz`/`alpha` scalars) and `joint_ladder/_ppc_montage_allexp.py` (dispatch: sharedzeta→`joint_ppc.band_chi2`, component→`model_overlay.solve_band` gain-marginal ridge, inlined since that module runs argv at import). **Key correction:** the `chosen` map (johndoeII C2D2) is the *mixed*-PBF selection — the all-exp campaign actually fit johndoeII as C2D1 (runbook L42), so **no refit was needed**; the authorized johndoeII C2D2 refit was unnecessary. whitney (fit locally as `whitney_fine`, C2D2 all-exp) was pushed to HPCC (242 MB data + path-rewritten configs + npz/json) to complete 7/7. **α match the runbook all-exp table; oran DSA χ² 5.32(base)→1.13(all-exp) — the base misfit was the PBF.** Honest fit-quality flags (figure-reviewer): **wilhelm DSA χ²=4.55 FAILS L2** (single sharedzeta comp misses DSA tail), whitney DSA 2.54 / CHIME 1.64 MARGINAL, phineas DSA 1.59 MARGINAL. Figures staged in scratchpad + `hpcc:/central/scratch/jfaber/flits-runs/data/joint/{tau_nu_ladder_allexp,joint_ppc_montage_allexp}.{pdf,svg,png}`; **NOT placed in `dsa_figs/`** (the committed PNGs there are base/mixed-PBF — left untouched). **Remaining @decision:** (a) commit the two generators in the refit lane; (b) #4 citable-α lock — whether wilhelm's poor DSA fit / the marginal bands stay in the published set; (c) manuscript placement into `dsa_figs/` + Faber2026 (one-way door, after #4). Runbook: `joint_ladder/ALLEXP_PBF_RUN.md` "Manuscript joint figures — regeneration". **@decision** — generation complete; placement gated on the citable-α lock (#4, ADR-0004 `ALPHA_MIN 1.5→1.0` regen).
- [x] **Extract the valid `fix/issue-4-n1-commensurable` commits — DONE 2026-06-24 (both merged to main).** Codex-adjudicated as a SPLIT off the local-only branch (whose tip carries closed #47's `7cae74e`, excluded from both): (1) issue-#4 acceptance test (`a76ca5e`) → **PR #56** (`test/issue-4-commensurable`, cherry-picked clean, 3/3 green); (2) s²/PBF guard (`1c87ef5`) → **PR #57** (`fix/s2-pbf-guard`). The guard's `test_fail_closed_separates_pbf_families` originally read the live dir and required untracked legacy mixed `*_s2-*.json` fixtures (absent on a clean checkout → fail); rewrote it to build a synthetic `tempfile` dir (one all-exp + one legacy-mixed record), arg-less so the `__main__` self-check still runs. Verified: `pytest test_s2verdict.py` 4 passed on clean base; mutation-killed (non-vacuous). `fix/issue-4-n1-commensurable` itself preserved as-is. Both PRs **merged to main 2026-06-24**; the HPCC all-exp grid pull (item above) is the only thing left before the guard can adjudicate zach C2D3.

- [ ] **Repair `analyze_scintillation_from_acfs` component extraction** (`scintillation/scint_analysis/analysis.py`, success branch ~L1389–1423). Confirmed 2026-06-24 (Codex review of the Phase-4 two-screen wiring): on a *successful* sub-band fit the loop builds `component_params` (a list of `(bw, mod, bw_err, mod_err)` **tuples**) but **never appends it to `params_per_comp`** — only the *failure* branch (~L1366–1369) appends (an empty `{}`). The downstream consumer (~L1430, `measurements = [p for p in params_list if "bw" in p]`) then expects **dicts** with keys `bw/mod/bw_err/mod_err/finite_err/gof`. Net effect: real `subband_measurements` are always empty (and the ODR power-law fit runs on empty arrays). This makes the Phase-4 wiring (`attach_scintillation_interpretation`) a no-op on *real* pipeline output even though its synthetic unit tests pass. The multi-component (`2c`/`3c`) path is also incomplete — it only ever builds one entry regardless of `num_comps`. **@decision** — fixing it correctly needs (a) the intended per-component dict contract (incl. where `finite_err` is plumbed from `calculate_acf`) and the `num_comps` layout — a design call, not a mechanical edit — and (b) real ACF data (gitignored, `DATA_SOURCES.md`) to validate end-to-end. Out of the incomplete-work-closeout Phase-4 scope (which only *wires* the interpretation funcs). Verify the failure on a real run (or a stored-fits path) before editing; the wiring itself is correct given the documented `final_results` contract.
- [x] **Push the Faber2026 `chime_subband_compare` fold** — committed `44db61a` on `draft/fork-b-finish` (budget.tex within-CHIME sub-band block + vector figure) and **pushed 2026-06-24** (`f324943..44db61a` → `origin/draft/fork-b-finish`, syncs to Overleaf).

content.text: Now a project memory note — the photoz-fix budget being canonical is durable context that affects all future budget work.

input.content:
---
name: photoz-fix-budget-canonical
description: Photo-z-corrected foreground/intervening DM budget (scratch/photoz-fix) is canonical over committed results/; manuscript reconciled 2026-06-24.
metadata:
  type: project
---

The intervening-galaxy DM budget existed in **three inconsistent states** in dsa110-FLITS (committed `results/`, `scratch/photoz-fix/`, `scratch/cluster-regen-out/`). On 2026-06-24 the user adopted **`scratch/photoz-fix/` as canonical**.

Under photo-z-fix the foreground census changes materially vs the old committed `budget_table.tex`:
- 3 galaxy-interior sightlines (b<0.1 R_vir): **FRB 20220310F (whitney) DM_int 364/200, FRB 20230307A (phineas) 733/471, FRB 20240229A (casey) 236/207** — Mahi/FRB 20230814B drops out (0 foreground galaxies now), Casey enters.
- CGM grazer: **FRB 20221113A (isha)** DM_int 112 (was 23).
- **Zach/FRB 20220207C drops to 0** (its 2 GLADE+ foreground galaxies are removed by the photo-z fix).
- Negative DM_host four: Whitney, Isha, Phineas, Casey.

The Faber2026 manuscript (`~/Developer/overleaf/Faber2026`) was reconciled to this state: `budget_table.tex`, the budget figure, and the interior-sightline narrative in results/discussion/conclusions were updated, and a new "dominant intervening systems" subsection + `grand_galaxies_cgm`/`grand_clusters_icm` figures were added (generator `galaxies/v2_0/grand_systems_figures.py`, has a self-check reproducing the budget). Record it (this clears the gate):

      --check "what you checked and how" --evidence "the concrete result: cmd+output / number / verdict"

Genuinely trivial edit (typo, comment, rename)? Log it, don't skip it silently:
